content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

September 8, 1997

Mr. Fred Kiani
2036 Euclid Avenue
East Palo Alto, CA 94303

Re: Your Request for Advice
    Our File No. I-97-449

Dear Mr. Kiani:

     This letter is a response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Because your request involves activities that have already
occurred, we are treating this as a request for informal assistance, as required under Regulation
18329 (copy enclosed). Please bear in mind that nothing in this letter should be construed as
an evaluation of any conduct which may already have taken place. Further, this letter is based on
the facts as they have been presented to us. The Commission does not act as a finder of fact in
providing advice. (In re Oglesby v (1975) 1 Fair Political Practices Commission Ops. 71.)

     In our phone conversation of August 25, 1997, you indicated that the Associated Property
Owners of East Palo Alto (APO) made independent expenditures in opposition to two ballot
measures for a special election held in July 1997; the money spent, however, was not raised for
political purposes. You also indicated that the total costs of the mailing exceeded $1,000.
Furthermore, you stated that the expenditures were made within 16 days before the election.

     In that same phone conversation, I told you that any organization that makes independent
expenditures aggregating $1,000 or more in a calendar year has a reporting obligation under the
Act. I also stated that when calculating the costs of a mailing which includes literature with
express advocacy supporting or opposing a ballot measure or candidate the costs of reproduction
for those pages which contain express advocacy plus the pro rata share of the mailing costs must
be included to determine the amount of the independent expenditure. Furthermore, I stated that
if an organization makes independent expenditures within 16 days before the election for which
the express advocacy was made, a late independent expenditure report was required.


¹ Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2,
sections 18109 - 18995, of the California Code of Regulations.

___________________________________________________________________________
P.O. BOX 27666, SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94127-0666    (415) 252-3666

File No. 1-97-449
Page 2

We have already sent you the Information Manual for Independent Expenditure
committees and a reporting form under separate cover. Please review these carefully to
determine what reports you are required to file. Even if a report is late, it must still be filed. If
you have not received the manual or reporting form, please contact either the city clerk for the
City of East Palo Alto or our offices for copies.

If you have any questions, please feel free to call me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: Kelvin S. Moen
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance Division

sections.question_synthetic: What are the reporting obligations for an organization that made independent expenditures aggregating over $1,000 opposing ballot measures within 16 days before a special election, using funds not originally raised for political purposes?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Any organization making independent expenditures of $1,000 or more in a calendar year must file reports under the Political Reform Act. When calculating mailing costs for express advocacy, the reproduction costs for pages with express advocacy plus the pro rata share of mailing costs must be included. If independent expenditures are made within 16 days before an election, a late independent expenditure report is required and must be filed even if late.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is an FPPC response letter treating the request as informal assistance under Regulation 18329. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections with headers; information extracted from letter body. Phone conversation from August 25, 1997 is referenced as context for the advice given.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What are the reporting obligations for an organization that made independent expenditures aggregating over $1,000 opposing ballot measures within 16 days before a special election, using funds not originally raised for political purposes?

CONCLUSION: Any organization making independent expenditures of $1,000 or more in a calendar year must file reports under the Political Reform Act. When calculating mailing costs for express advocacy, the reproduction costs for pages with express advocacy plus the pro rata share of mailing costs must be included. If independent expenditures are made within 16 days before an election, a late independent expenditure report is required and must be filed even if late.

embedding.summary: FPPC response to Fred Kiani regarding independent expenditure reporting obligations for the Associated Property Owners of East Palo Alto, which made expenditures over $1,000 opposing two ballot measures within 16 days before a July 1997 special election.
